hub.title: LLM Capabilities and Real-Time Model Comparisons: A Framework for Choosing the Right Model
hub.topicEntity: Real-time large language model capability evaluation
hub.summary: Defines a practical, repeatable framework to compare LLMs in real time across quality, latency, cost, tool-use, reasoning reliability, safety behavior, and domain fit—then maps those evaluation dimensions to structured decision criteria and reporting standards.

spokes.title: Benchmarking LLM Capabilities with Real-Time Evaluation
spokes.summary: Covers how to design real-time evaluation runs (prompt sets, test harnesses, success metrics, confidence estimation, and change tracking) to compare model performance fairly under production-like conditions.
spokes.anchorToHub: real-time benchmarking methodology for comparing LLM capabilities fairly
spokes.hubAnchorToSpoke: benchmarking LLM capabilities with real-time evaluation

spokes.title: Latency, Cost, and Throughput Tradeoffs in LLM Choice
spokes.summary: Explains how to measure and compare latency distributions, tokens per second, throughput, and unit cost—then translate those findings into model selection for interactive, batch, and high-concurrency workflows.
spokes.anchorToHub: latency, cost, and throughput tradeoffs that drive real-time model choice
spokes.hubAnchorToSpoke: latency, cost, and throughput tradeoffs in LLM choice

spokes.title: Tool-Use and Agentic Reliability Across LLMs
spokes.topicEntity: Tool invocation and multi-step agent reliability
spokes.summary: Details how to evaluate tool calling accuracy, planning-to-execution consistency, multi-step success rates, error recovery, and hallucination risk when models are integrated with functions, search, or retrieval systems.
spokes.anchorToHub: tool-use and agentic reliability evaluation across LLMs
spokes.hubAnchorToSpoke: tool-use and agentic reliability across LLMs
